title: What to know about high cholesterol before treating it with medicinal herbs
intro_desc: High cholesterol is a condition where there are elevated levels of cholesterol in the blood, which can lead to the formation of plaque in the arteries, increasing the risk of heart disease. Negative impacts include chest pain, heart attack, and stroke. Causes include an unhealthy diet, lack of exercise, obesity, and genetic factors. Medicinal herbs that can help lower cholesterol include garlic, artichoke, turmeric, and psyllium. Garlic can be consumed raw or in supplement form, artichoke can be consumed as a vegetable or in supplement form, turmeric can be consumed as a spice or in supplement form, and psyllium can be consumed as a fiber supplement. Precautions include consulting with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, as some herbs can interact with medications and have side effects.
definition: High cholesterol is a condition where you have too much of a fatty substance called cholesterol in your blood. This excess cholesterol can lead to the formation of plaques in your arteries, causing them to narrow and harden, a condition known as atherosclerosis. This can result in various negative consequences on your health and quality of life. For instance, high cholesterol can increase your risk of heart disease, heart attacks, and strokes, which can be life-threatening. Moreover, it can lead to peripheral artery disease, where your limbs, particularly your legs, don't receive enough blood and oxygen, causing pain, cramping, and difficulty walking. Additionally, high cholesterol can cause gallstones and even lead to memory loss, dementia, and depression. Overall, uncontrolled high cholesterol can significantly impair your health, well-being, and longevity, making it essential to monitor and manage it effectively.
herbs_desc: The best medicinal herbs for high cholesterol are lemon balm, oregano, garlic, ginger, and gotu kola. Lemon balm has been shown to help lower cholesterol levels due to its antioxidant properties. Oregano contains carvacrol, a compound that has been found to reduce cholesterol absorption in the gut. Garlic has been widely studied for its ability to lower cholesterol and triglyceride levels, as well as improve blood pressure. Ginger has been shown to reduce cholesterol levels by increasing the excretion of bile acids and reducing the synthesis of cholesterol in the liver. Gotu kola, an herb commonly used in traditional medicine, has been found to lower cholesterol and triglyceride levels, as well as improve blood flow. These herbs offer a natural and holistic approach to managing high cholesterol levels and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
herbs_list: Lemon balm: Contains antioxidants that have been shown to lower LDL cholesterol levels.
Oregano: Has been found to help reduce cholesterol absorption in the gut.
Garlic: Known for its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve heart health.
Ginger: May help lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease.
Gotu kola: May help reduce cholesterol levels and improve blood circulation.
Siberian ginseng: Can help lower cholesterol levels and improve overall heart health.
Licorice: May help lower cholesterol levels, but should be used with caution due to potential side effects.
Milk thistle: Can help lower cholesterol levels and protect the liver from damage.
Fenugreek: Has been shown to help lower cholesterol levels and improve heart health.
White tea: Contains antioxidants that can help lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease.
